title: Binary Tree Paths
description: Given a binary tree, return all root-to-leaf paths. A leaf is a node with no children. Each path should be represented as a string in the format: "node1->node2->node3". The order of paths in the output does not matter.

examples.explanation: The binary tree can be visualized as: ``` 1 / \ 2 3 \ 5 ``` The root-to-leaf paths are: - From 1 to 2 to 5: "1->2->5" - From 1 to 3: "1->3"
In this case, the tree consists of a single node: ``` 1 ``` The only path is from the root to itself: "1".
An empty tree has no nodes and hence no paths. The output is an empty list.

constraints: The number of nodes in the tree is in the range [0, 100].
Node values are in the range [-1000, 1000].
hints: Consider using depth-first search to explore each path from root to leaves.
You may need to store the current path as you traverse the tree.
Don't forget to handle the case where the tree is empty.
Try to backtrack after reaching a leaf node to explore other paths.
